_description: Manually-authored sparse bone correspondences for all 12 absent eval pairs. Authoring rationale: anatomical analogue mapping using the contact_groups JSON + cond.npy joint names. Format: each pair has 'pairs' (list of {src_joint_idx, tgt_joint_idx, semantic} tuples), and an 'anchor_motion' on the target skeleton chosen as the closest-action surrogate.
_author: orchestrator (claude opus 4.6) via cond.npy joint-names + contact_groups.json + anatomical reasoning

pair_10_SabreToothTiger_jump_to_Parrot.anchor_motion_rationale: Parrot cannot jump-quadruped-style. Map quadruped jump takeoff rhythm to parrot wing-down-thrust + feet-push rhythm. Use Parrot's Walk or Hop clip as anchor (periodic foot-leave rhythm).

pairs.semantic: Hips ↔ Hips
SabreTiger LF (front-left hand-region used for jump takeoff lift) ↔ Parrot LW wing-base (push-down during flap/leap)
SabreTiger RF ↔ Parrot RW wing-base
SabreTiger LH foot (rear-left thrust) ↔ Parrot L foot (push-off)
SabreTiger RH foot ↔ Parrot R foot

pair_11_Ostrich_walk_to_Crocodile.anchor_motion_rationale: Crocodile has no Walk clip (absent). Use crocodile's Idle (body low, legs in contact) as substrate; ostrich walking rhythm maps onto crocodile's slow forward-rotational locomotion.

pairs.semantic: Hips ↔ Hips
Ostrich L foot (bipedal left-leg stride) ↔ Crocodile LF (front-left limb; takes walking phase)
Ostrich R foot ↔ Crocodile RF
Ostrich LW wing-base (stabilisation during walk) ↔ Crocodile LH (rear-left limb, complement of front limb phase)
Ostrich RW wing-base ↔ Crocodile RH

pair_12_Comodoa_attack_to_Puppy.anchor_motion_rationale: Puppy has no attack clip. Use Run or Jump as anchor (aggressive lunging motion). Both are quadrupeds so direct LF/RF/LH/RH mapping.

pairs.semantic: Hips ↔ Hips
Comodoa LF ↔ Puppy LF (front-left paw)
Comodoa RF ↔ Puppy RF
Comodoa LH ↔ Puppy LH
Comodoa RH ↔ Puppy RH

pair_16_Isopetra_attack_to_Flamingo.anchor_motion_rationale: Flamingo has no attack clip; use Flamingo Fly or Stand as anchor (wing-strike as aggressive motion surrogate). Multi-leg isopod → bipedal flyer is extreme morphology gap.

pairs.semantic: Hips ↔ Hips
Isopetra LF (front-most left leg) ↔ Flamingo LW wing-base (left wing = front-left strike appendage)
Isopetra RF ↔ Flamingo RW
Isopetra LH (rearmost left) ↔ Flamingo LF foot (left leg, body anchor)
Isopetra RH ↔ Flamingo RF

pair_18_Centipede_run_to_Giantbee.anchor_motion_rationale: Giantbee has no Run clip; use Fly or RapidFly (rhythmic wing-beat) as anchor. Centipede metachronal wave maps onto bee's wing + leg rhythm.

pairs.semantic: Hips ↔ Hips
Centipede LF (front-most left leg) ↔ Giantbee LF
Centipede RF ↔ Giantbee RF
Centipede L_mid1 (front-mid-left leg drives metachronal wave phase) ↔ Giantbee LW (left wing drives flight rhythm)
Centipede R_mid1 ↔ Giantbee RW
Centipede LH ↔ Giantbee LH

pair_19_Bird_idle_to_Pteranodon.anchor_motion_rationale: Pteranodon has no Idle; use Glide or LowSoar (wing-settled-open, body still) as anchor. Both flyers, wing anatomy parallels.

pairs.semantic: Hips ↔ Hips
Bird LF ↔ Pteranodon LF
Bird RF ↔ Pteranodon RF
Bird LW ↔ Pteranodon LW (semi-settled wings during idle)
Bird RW ↔ Pteranodon RW

pair_26_Ostrich_idle_to_HermitCrab.anchor_motion_rationale: HermitCrab has no Idle; use Slowwalk (minimal body displacement, occasional leg-tap) as closest-to-idle anchor.

pairs.semantic: Hips ↔ Hips
Ostrich L foot (standing left foot) ↔ HermitCrab L1 (front-left leg for standing support)
Ostrich R foot ↔ HermitCrab R1
Ostrich LW wing-base (folded-rest) ↔ HermitCrab claw_L (left claw held at-rest)
Ostrich RW wing-base ↔ HermitCrab claw_R

pair_13_Crab_eat_to_PolarBear.anchor_motion_rationale: PolarBear has no eat-labeled clip directly (absent); use idle as closest semantic since 'eat' for a crab is sit-and-pinch with claws, which maps to bear standing-with-front-paw motion

pairs.semantic: Hips ↔ Hips (root anchor)
Crab claw_L (left big claw, manipulation organ) ↔ PolarBear RF Bip01_R_Hand-region (front paw used for manipulation)
Crab claw_R ↔ PolarBear LF (mirror to maintain L-R symmetry)
Crab R1 leg-tip (front-most right leg) ↔ PolarBear RH Bip01_R_Foot (rear paw, ground-contact)
Crab L1 leg-tip ↔ PolarBear LH Bip01_L_Foot (rear paw, ground-contact)

pair_14_Comodoa_walk_to_Eagle.anchor_motion_rationale: Eagle is primarily a flyer; walk is rare. Pick eagle's ground-locomotion clip (likely Idle or Land), since absent regime means no direct walk match exists

pairs.semantic: Comodoa Hips ↔ Eagle Hips (root)
Comodoa LF Bip01_L_Foot (front-left foot used for walking) ↔ Eagle LF Bip01_L_Foot (left foot, used for grounded walking)
Comodoa RF Bip01_R_Foot ↔ Eagle RF Bip01_R_Foot
Comodoa LF (drives the left-front gait phase) ↔ Eagle LW Bip01_L_Wing-base (eagle's left wing fills the spatial role of front-left limb during walk)
Comodoa RF ↔ Eagle RW (right wing as front-right limb spatial-role)

pair_15_Goat_eat_to_Ant.anchor_motion_rationale: Ant has no eat clip; eating for goat is head-down-still motion which best maps to ant idle-still

pairs.semantic: Goat Bip01_Spine ↔ Ant Bip01_Spine (body-axis anchor)
Goat Bip01_Head ↔ Ant Bip01_Spine (ant has no head joint; spine carries head motion in 6-leg insects)
Goat LH Bip01_L_Foot ↔ Ant LH Bip01_L_Foot-region (rear-left foot, mid-leg contact)
Goat RH Bip01_R_Foot ↔ Ant RH (rear-right)
Goat LF Bip01_L_Hand ↔ Ant LF (front-left leg in ant's 6-leg body)
Goat RF Bip01_R_Hand ↔ Ant RF (front-right leg in ant's 6-leg body)

pair_17_Parrot_fly_to_PolarBearB.anchor_motion_rationale: PolarBearB cannot fly. Fly action for parrot is rhythmic wing-flapping; most semantically-close target motion is rhythmic gait (walk) where front legs swing periodically. Use a Walk clip as anchor; wing-flap rhythm gets mapped onto front-leg swing rhythm

pairs.semantic: Parrot Bip01_Spine ↔ PolarBearB Bip01_Spine (body anchor)
Parrot LW Bip01_L_Wing-base ↔ PolarBearB LF Bip01_L_Hand-region (left wing rhythm → left front-leg rhythm)
Parrot RW Bip01_R_Wing-base ↔ PolarBearB RF Bip01_R_Hand-region (right wing → right front-leg)
Parrot L Bip01_L_Foot (parrot foot, retracted during fly) ↔ PolarBearB LH Bip01_L_Foot (rear-left foot)
Parrot R Bip01_R_Foot ↔ PolarBearB RH Bip01_R_Foot

pair_27_Buzzard_fly_to_Centipede.anchor_motion_rationale: Centipede cannot fly; closest periodic motion is Run (rhythmic many-leg metachronal wave). Wing-flap rhythm maps onto leg-wave rhythm

pairs.semantic: Buzzard Bip01_Spine ↔ Centipede root/Hips (body axis anchor)
Buzzard LW Bip01_L_Wing ↔ Centipede L_mid1 (front-left mid-segment leg, drives one phase of metachronal wave)
Buzzard RW Bip01_R_Wing ↔ Centipede R_mid1 (front-right mid-segment leg)
Buzzard LF Bip01_L_Foot (buzzard foot retracts during flight) ↔ Centipede LF (front-most left leg)
Buzzard RF Bip01_R_Foot ↔ Centipede RF (front-most right leg)
